steps.canonical_text: Call emergency services now. Ask any bystander to bring an AED if one is nearby.
Place the heel of one hand on the center of the chest, on the lower half of the breastbone. Place the other hand on top and interlock the fingers.
Push hard and fast: about 5 centimeters deep at a rate of 100 to 120 per minute. Let the chest fully rise between pushes.
When an AED arrives, turn it on and follow the voice prompts. Keep doing compressions while pads are attached.
Do not stop compressions. If another helper is available, switch every two minutes to avoid fatigue. Continue until emergency services arrive or the person starts breathing normally.
